info.title: Processing
info.description: Configure Engines and Clusters, manage and browse executions of your Tasks, Plans and Promotions.

get.description:
Get task executions. Available filters are :

* some specific environment as the alternate filter to workspaces
* some specific workspace as the alternate filter to environments
* status of execution
* limit to a number of days in recent history (for example, 1 : yesterday, 5 : past five days)
* datetime period in the past, not exceeding a 60-day range

All those parameters are optional and can be combined at your convenience. Note that the workspace should belong to the environment when both filters (environmentId and workspaceId) are specified in the same call.

When neither the number of days nor a datetime period is provided, the default 60 days time range is applied.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Time period filter start date time (milliseconds).    
With the `from` and `to` time period filters, the executions to be included in the response are:
* Executions that started within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that ended within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that started and ended within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that ran throughout the entire specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.

schema.description:
Time period filter start date time (milliseconds).    
With the `from` and `to` time period filters, the executions to be included in the response are:
* Executions that started within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that ended within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that started and ended within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that ran throughout the entire specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0

schema.description: Position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0

parameters.name: to
parameters.description:
Time period filter end date time (milliseconds).    
With the `from` and `to` time period filters, the executions to be included in the response are:
* Executions that started within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that ended within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that started and ended within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that ran throughout the entire specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.

With the `from` and `to` time period filters, the executions to be included in the response are:

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100

schema.description: Number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter on status of execution: | Status | Execution status | |-----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | dispatching | EXECUTION_EVENT_RECEIVED, DISPATCHING_FLOW | | deploy_failed | DEPLOY_FAILED | | executing | STARTING_FLOW_EXECUTION | | execution_successful | EXECUTION_SUCCESS | | execution_rejected | EXECUTION_REJECTED | | execution_failed | EXECUTION_FAILED | | terminated | EXECUTION_TERMINATED with **any sub status** | | terminated_timeout | EXECUTION_TERMINATED with **sub status TIMEOUT** | | terminated_shutdown | EXECUTION_TERMINATED with **sub status SHUTDOWN** |

schema.description: Filter on status of execution: | Status | Execution status | |-----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | dispatching | EXECUTION_EVENT_RECEIVED, DISPATCHING_FLOW | | deploy_failed | DEPLOY_FAILED | | executing | STARTING_FLOW_EXECUTION | | execution_successful | EXECUTION_SUCCESS | | execution_rejected | EXECUTION_REJECTED | | execution_failed | EXECUTION_FAILED | | terminated | EXECUTION_TERMINATED with **any sub status** | | terminated_timeout | EXECUTION_TERMINATED with **sub status TIMEOUT** | | terminated_shutdown | EXECUTION_TERMINATED with **sub status SHUTDOWN** |

post.description:
Get available task executions. Available filters are :

* some specific environment as the alternate filter to workspaces
* some specific workspace as the alternate filter to environments
*  status of execution
* tags associated with tasks : when multiple tags are provided, each of them will be used as a filter, that is to say, the logical AND is applied
* limit to a number of days in recent history (for example, 1 : yesterday, 5 : past five days)
* datetime period in the past, not exceeding a 60-day range

All those parameters are optional and can be combined at your convenience. Note that the workspace should belong to the environment when both filters (environmentId and workspaceId) are specified in the same call.

When neither the number of days nor a datetime period is provided, the default 60 days time range is applied.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Time period filter start date time (milliseconds).    
With the `from` and `to` time period filters, the executions to be included in the response are:
* Executions that started within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that ended within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that started and ended within the specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.
* Executions that ran throughout the entire specified timeframe between `from` and `to`.

post.description:
Allows to run a Plan. 

This endpoint also allows for re-running some plan execution from some failed step : you should then provide execution id and step from which you wish to restart execution.

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

405.description: Plan is paused, it can’t be executed

422.description: Entity exists but cannot be processed, because too old or plan exec didn't fail

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

501.description: Plan cannot be deployed because it is not configured

get.description:
List all plan executions the user is granted visibility on. 
For each plan execution returns execution and plan identifiers, start and finish timestamps, execution status and counters on done and planned executables within plan. 
This query is paginated and accepts filters as defined in parameters documented above.

post.description:
Execute a Promotion from source Environment to target one, according to all the default configuration set on Tasks.  

Detailed rules on promotion executions are available here : https://help.talend.com/r/en-US/Cloud/management-console-user-guide/promotion-rules

In addition to these rules, current execution can be adapted as such : 
* Default scope of Promotion is the whole environment. When narrower scope (Workspace, Task, etc.) please provide advanced option parameter accordingly. 
* Run Profiles and Resources are defaulty updated on target. You can also choose to keep them untouched using parameters. 

Lastly, [promotion simulation and analysis](/apis/orchestration/2021-03/#operation_perform-promotion-analysis) is also available: helpful when check before exec is needed. Automatic-undo of a promotion execution is not provided.

501.description: Promotions can't be executed because it has errors

delete.description:
Unpair Remote Engine.

If data service tasks and Route tasks are available in your license, you can maintain OSGi Data Services as deployed while upgrading your Remote Engine, without need to undeploy and redeploy those services. See [Upgrading Remote Engine v2.11.8 onwards for data service Job or Route tasks](https://help.talend.com/r/en-US/Cloud/installation-guide-linux/upgrading-remote-engine-for-esb-tasks) for more details about this specific upgrade process.

parameters.name: keepOsgiDeployments
parameters.description: Boolean to indicate if OSGi Routes or data services should be kept as deployed while unpairing - used for Remote Engine upgrade with data service tasks and Route tasks only

schema.description: Boolean to indicate if OSGi Routes or data services should be kept as deployed while unpairing - used for Remote Engine upgrade with data service tasks and Route tasks only

parameters.name: keepMicroserviceDeployments
parameters.description: Boolean to indicate if microservice Routes or data services should be kept as deployed while unpairing - used for Remote Engine upgrade with data service tasks and Route tasks only

schema.description: Boolean to indicate if microservice Routes or data services should be kept as deployed while unpairing - used for Remote Engine upgrade with data service tasks and Route tasks only

delete.description:
Delete engine run profile. 

When in use in some task configuration, deletion is aborted unless you provided some new Run Profile id to use. It that latter case, tasks will be using this other Run Profile and the original Run Profile would be deleted.

Results are sorted alphabetically by name and then by the most recent update.

Results are sorted alphabetically by name and then by the most recent update.

processorIds.description: List of processor id  (engineId / clusterId / dynamicEngineId)

limit.description: Number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100

offset.description: Position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0

sortBy.description: Specifies the field by which results are sorted.

sortOrder.description:
Determines the sorting direction of query results.
- Allowed values:
   - `ASC`: Sorts results in ascending order (A to Z, 0 to 9)
   - `DESC`: Sorts results in descending order (Z to A, 9 to 0)

timeout.description: Execution timeout in minutes. Minimal value is 1.

userId.description: User who triggered or scheduled the execution

userType.description: Type of user who triggered or scheduled the execution

jobVersion.description: The version of the executed job

executionStatus.description: Execution status: | Status | Meaning | |-------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | EXECUTION_EVENT_RECEIVED | The system has received a request to execute a task, but processing has not yet started. | | DISPATCHING_FLOW | The system is assigning the task to a processor. | | STARTING_FLOW_EXECUTION | The task execution is being initialized by the process and resources are being allocated. | | STOPPING_FLOW_EXECUTION | The task execution is in the process of being stopped. | | EXECUTION_TERMINATED | The task execution was stopped by the user or system before completion. | | EXECUTION_TERMINATED_TIMEOUT | The task execution was automatically terminated due to exceeding the allowed time limit. | | DEPLOY_FAILED | The task could not be deployed to the processor. | | EXECUTION_FAILED | The task execution encountered an error and did not complete successfully. | | EXECUTION_REJECTED | The task executed successfully but marked as rejected because it contains rejected rows. | | EXECUTION_SUCCESS | The task executed successfully. | | EXECUTION_TERMINATED_SHUTDOWN | The task execution was terminated due to a system shutdown. |

apiVersion.description:
The version of the API that was used to run the task.  
Only available if the task was executed through API call to `/processing` API, If the task was executed by webhook/schedule/plan/UI this field will be absent.  
Format: `YYYY-MM`  
_since `2026-01`_

executionDestination.description:
Destination of execution  
_Note: `ELASTIC_ENGINE` is legacy name of `PIPELINE_ENGINE`_

status.description: Task execution status: | Status | Execution status | |-----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | dispatching | EXECUTION_EVENT_RECEIVED, DISPATCHING_FLOW | | deploy_failed | DEPLOY_FAILED | | executing | STARTING_FLOW_EXECUTION | | execution_successful | EXECUTION_SUCCESS | | execution_rejected | EXECUTION_REJECTED | | execution_failed | EXECUTION_FAILED | | terminated | EXECUTION_TERMINATED with **any sub status** | | terminated_timeout | EXECUTION_TERMINATED with **sub status TIMEOUT** | | terminated_shutdown | EXECUTION_TERMINATED with **sub status SHUTDOWN** |

Execution info  
*Note: it's preferable to use **JobExecutionStatus** as they are equal*

executionStatus.description: Execution status: | Status | Meaning | |-------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | EXECUTION_EVENT_RECEIVED | The system has received a request to execute a task, but processing has not yet started. | | DISPATCHING_FLOW | The system is assigning the task to a processor. | | STARTING_FLOW_EXECUTION | The task execution is being initialized by the process and resources are being allocated. | | STOPPING_FLOW_EXECUTION | The task execution is in the process of being stopped. | | EXECUTION_TERMINATED | The task execution was stopped by the user or system before completion. | | EXECUTION_TERMINATED_TIMEOUT | The task execution was automatically terminated due to exceeding the allowed time limit. | | DEPLOY_FAILED | The task could not be deployed to the processor. | | EXECUTION_FAILED | The task execution encountered an error and did not complete successfully. | | EXECUTION_REJECTED | The task executed successfully but marked as rejected because it contains rejected rows. | | EXECUTION_SUCCESS | The task executed successfully. | | EXECUTION_TERMINATED_SHUTDOWN | The task execution was terminated due to a system shutdown. |

protectedArtifactUpdate.description:
Allows to restrict the scope of task updates when a new artifact version is published. 
- true : the tasks of this workspace are protected from the unintended updates triggered by artifacts being published in other workspaces. These tasks are updated only when artifacts are being published in the same workspace
- false : tasks that have been set to use the latest version of the published artifact, or any other artifact that has the same name as the newly published artifact, are all updated, whatever workspace is used for this artifact publishing

BigDataRunProfile.description: Pipeline run profile of type 'Big Data'

checkPointFilePath.description: Checkpoint file path. Required if checkpointing enabled

token.description: Token (can also be provided in the Remote Engine configuration)

id.description: ID of existing cluster. Required for 'old' cluster

sparkVersion.description: Databricks runtime version. Required for 'new' cluster

nodeTypeId.description: Node type ID. Required for 'new' cluster

workersNumber.description: Number of workers. Required for 'new' cluster

jvmArguments.description: jvm arguments applied on job execution

createDate.description: Date of creation of the resource

updateDate.description: Date of updating of the resource

managed.description: Indicates whether target runtime (engine/cluster) is managed or not

pushdownPreferenceEnabled.description: Prefer executions in SQL Pushdown mode when possible (if pipelines are compatible, they are sent to the data warehouse for execution).

warehouseName.description: Optional warehouse name you can provide to overwrite the default warehouse parameter used to connect to the data warehouse at runtime.

pushdownPreferenceEnabled.description: Prefer executions in SQL Pushdown mode when possible (if pipelines are compatible, they are sent to the data warehouse for execution).

warehouseName.description: Optional warehouse name you can provide to overwrite the default warehouse parameter used to connect to the data warehouse at runtime.

initialExecutors.description: Initial number of executors (for Dynamic strategy)

minExecutors.description: Min. number of executors (for Dynamic strategy)

maxExecutors.description: Max. number of executors (for Dynamic strategy)

scopeId.description: ID of the environment in which this run profile is defined. A run profile is defined either directly in a Dynamic Engine environment or through Remote Engine or Cluster settings.

workspaceId.description: Id of the workspace this run profile is limited to (optional). Not provided in the case of Dynamic Engine.

processorId.description: Id of the processor (RE / RE Cluster / Gen2 / Dynamic Engine)

DIRunProfile.description: Run profile that can be used for Data Integration job running on dynamic engines, remote engines or cluster of remote engines

runtimeId.description: Either the Remote Engine id or the Remote Engine cluster id.

createDate.description: Date of creation of the resource

updateDate.description: Date of updating of the resource

managed.description: Indicates whether target runtime (engine/cluster) is managed or not

description.description: Environment description (not returned when querying a list of objects)

detail.example: Token authorization is required for access

requestId.description: The unique identification of the request involved with this error

Public.description:
This Bearer authentication can be set in the Authorization header of your requests.
Authentication tokens and Personal Access Tokens are supported.
Personal Access Tokens can be generated in the Profile Preferences page of the Talend Cloud Portal.
